title: 10 best herbal creams for shingles

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Calendula cream has been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritation associated with shingles, providing relief from pain and discomfort.

Antiviral properties: Pot marigold contains compounds that have antiviral activity against herpes viruses, which are responsible for causing shingles, potentially helping to shorten the duration and severity of the outbreak.

Wound-healing properties: Calendula's antibacterial and antifungal properties promote wound healing by preventing infection and promoting collagen synthesis, which can help speed up the recovery process from shingle-related skin lesions.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream soothes and calms the affected skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with shingles. The antioxidants present in Calendula officinalis help to promote wound healing, reducing the risk of post-herpetic neuralgia, a common complication of shingles. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties may help to alleviate the pain and discomfort caused by shingles, promoting faster recovery and relief.
remedy_constituents: Carotenoids: They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce redness and itching associated with shingles.
Flavonoids (specifically, luteolin and apigenin): These phenolic compounds exhibit antiviral and anti-inflammatory activities, which may help combat the herpes zoster virus that causes shingles.

Sesquiterpenes (specifically, calendulic acid and isocalendulic acid): They have potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of secondary infections often seen in shingles patients.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Calendula flowers are the most used part in creams for shingles because of their anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help soothe and heal shingles blisters.
Leaves: Calendula leaves contain a high concentration of antioxidants, which aid in reducing pain and inflammation associated with shingles.
Roots: The roots of Calendula officinalis contain a high concentration of saponins, which have antiviral properties that help fight the shingles virus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried calendula flowers from a trusted source in a clean environment taking about 2 hours.
Combine 1 cup of dried calendula flowers with 2 cups of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks or 14 days, shaking daily for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh sieve to separate the liquid from solids taking about 30 minutes.
Mix 1/4 cup of the infused liquid with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and alocemide, help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with shingles rash.
Antiviral properties: The anthraquinone glycosides in aloe vera have been shown to inhibit the replication of herpes viruses, including varicella-zoster virus (VZV), which causes shingles.

Wound-healing properties: Aloe vera's gel contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as amino acids and enzymes, which promote collagen synthesis, improve skin elasticity, and accelerate the healing process of shingles sores.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The gel extracted from the plant has been used for centuries to soothe and calm irritated skin. When applied topically, aloe vera cream can help reduce the pain, itching, and discomfort associated with shingles. Its cooling effect can also help to reduce inflammation and promote the healing process, providing relief and comfort to those affected by the shingles rash.
remedy_constituents: Glycoproteins: These help in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, which can aid in the recovery process from shingles.
Acemannan: This polysaccharide constituent has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which can help in alleviating the pain and discomfort associated with shingles.

Vitamins and minerals (especially Vitamin A and E, and Calcium): These antioxidants and minerals have anti-inflammatory properties, promote skin healing, and help in reducing the severity and duration of shingles symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of aloin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritations.
Gel from Leaves: The gel extracted from Aloe vera leaves is widely used for its anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties, which can help to alleviate shingles symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Extract 100 grams of aloe vera gel from 2-3 aloe vera leaves using a vegetable peeler.
Melt 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Combine 50 grams of coconut oil and 25 grams of shea butter in a heat-proof bowl.
Stir the melted beeswax mixture into the coconut oil mixture until well combined and smooth.
Add 10 grams of vitamin e oil and 5 grams of lavender essential oil to the mixture and mix well.

remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has potent antiviral activity, which helps to reduce the replication of the varicella-zoster virus (VZV) that causes shingles.
Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and pain associated with shingles.

Tannins: St. John's Wort creams also contain tannins, which have astringent and antiseptic properties that can help to reduce itching, inflammation, and prevent bacterial or fungal infections in the affected area.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St. John's Wort, creams helps with shingles because of its antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation associated with shingles. These compounds may also help to shorten the duration of the shingles outbreak and reduce the risk of postherpetic neuralgia, a complication of shingles that can cause chronic pain. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties may help to soothe and calm the affected skin.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Hyperforin is a terpene that has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help reduce the severity and duration of shingles symptoms.
Hypericin: Hypericin is a naphthodianthron compound that has potent antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate shingles pain and reduce the risk of postherpetic neuralgia.

Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin and Kaempferol): These flavonoids are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce shingles pain, inflammation, and promote wound healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in hyperforin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help in soothing shingles symptoms.
Flowers: They contain flavonoids and hyperforin, which contribute to their antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects, making them suitable for treating shingles.
Stems: They are a rich source of hyperforin, which helps in reducing pain and inflammation associated with shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 100g of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves from a clean source.
Combine the dried plant material with 100g of sweet almond oil in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture gently for 30 minutes on low heat to infuse the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Add 20g of beeswax to the infused oil and heat for a further 10 minutes to melt.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compound, apigenin, helps reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with shingles rash, making it more comfortable for patients.

Antiviral property: Chamomile contains flavonoids and terpenoids that have been shown to inhibit the replication of herpes viruses, including the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles, which can help reduce the severity and duration of the outbreak.

Soothing and calming property: Chamomile's gentle, non-irritating nature helps calm and soothe irritated skin, reducing itchiness, burning sensations, and discomfort associated with shingles rash, allowing for a more peaceful recovery.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation associated with shingles. The cream's ability to calm and soothe the skin can also aid in reducing the severity and duration of shingle outbreaks. Additionally, chamomile's antimicrobial properties can help prevent secondary infections, promoting a faster healing process and reducing the risk of further complications.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with shingles.
Alpha-bisabolol: A sesquiterpene alcohol that has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, helping to soothe and calm the affected skin.
Matricine: A sesquiterpene lactone that has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce the severity and duration of shingles symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make creams for shingles due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and calm the affected area.
Seeds: They are used to make creams for shingles due to their antiviral and antifungal properties, which help combat the underlying causes of shingles.
Leaves: They are used to make creams for shingles due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce pain and promote healing in the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 25g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in 500ml of cold-pressed oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
In a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water, melt 100g of beeswax.
Combine the infused oil and melted beeswax, then heat the mixture to 160-180 degrees fahrenheit for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the cream from the heat and let it cool and solidify before whipping it to a smooth consistency.

remedy_properties: Antiviral and antibacterial properties: English lavender has been shown to exhibit antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can help combat the virus responsible for shingles (Varicella-Zoster Virus) and prevent bacterial infections from developing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with shingles blisters, making it a valuable ingredient in creams designed to alleviate symptoms.

Sedative and calming effects: Lavender oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help soothe anxiety and promote relaxation, both of which are common complaints among people suffering from shingles.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce pain and itching associated with shingles. By soothing the skin and promoting relaxation, these creams can help alleviate the discomfort and stress caused by shingles, allowing the body to recover more efficiently. Additionally, their antimicrobial properties can aid in preventing secondary infections, further supporting the healing process.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate the pain and inflammation associated with shingles.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene has antiviral and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce the severity and duration of shingles by targeting the underlying viral infection.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing in shingles patients.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used due to their antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties, which can help soothe and calm shingles pain and inflammation.
Leaves: Utilized for their antiseptic and antifungal properties, which can aid in preventing infection and promoting wound healing.
Flowers (again): Also used due to their calming effect, which can help reduce stress and anxiety associated with shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak potency for maximum oil extraction.
Steep 1 cup of the dried flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container to separate oil from solids.
Mix 1/2 cup of the extracted oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it thickens and cools into a smooth, creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Tea tree oil has antiviral properties that can help combat the varicella-zoster virus, which causes shingles, reducing the severity and duration of the outbreak.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal tea tree creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with shingles rash, promoting faster healing and relief from discomfort.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties in herbal tea tree creams help prevent bacterial infections that can occur when the skin is broken due to shingles lesions, reducing the risk of complications and promoting a healthy recovery.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with shingles because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredient, melaleuca oil, has been shown to reduce the severity of shingles symptoms, including pain, itching, and blistering. It achieves this by inhibiting the growth of the herpes zoster virus, which causes shingles, and reducing inflammation and swelling. As a result, tea tree creams can provide quick relief and promote faster healing for those suffering from shingles.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): It helps with shingles by reducing inflammation and relieving pain due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Cymene: It has antiviral properties, which help combat the virus responsible for shingles (herpes zoster) and reduce the severity of symptoms.
Terpinen-4-ol: It possesses antimicrobial properties, which help prevent secondary infections that can occur in shingles sores, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in essential oils, particularly tea tree oil, which has antiviral and antifungal properties that help combat shingles symptoms.
Rhyzomes: They contain melaleuca oil, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe and heal shingles lesions.
Stems: They also contain melaleuca oil, which helps reduce inflammation and promote wound healing in shingles patients.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves on a sunny morning after gentle rain.
Steep the harvested leaves in 500 milliliters of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Combine the strained liquid with 50 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture until it thickens and reaches 35 to 40 degrees celsius in about 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with shingles by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Antiviral: The active compounds in ginger, particularly gingerols and paradols, have antiviral properties that can help combat the herpes virus responsible for shingles, reducing the severity and duration of the outbreak.

Pain-relieving: Ginger's analgesic and anesthetic properties, which are due to its ability to inhibit pain-sensing neurons, provide relief from the intense pain associated with shingles, making it a natural alternative to opioid-based medications.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation associated with shingles. Additionally, ginger has been found to enhance blood circulation, which can aid in the healing process of shingles sores. By applying a Zingiber officinale cream topically, individuals may experience relief from shingles symptoms, including pain and itching.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, particularly [6]-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with shingles.

Shogaols: Shogaols, such as [6]-shogaol, exhibit antiviral and anti-inflammatory activities, which may help combat the herpes zoster virus that causes shingles and alleviate symptoms like itching and burning.
Zingiberene: Zingiberene, a sesquiterpene, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate pain associated with shingles.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Zingiber officinale rhyzomes are used to make creams for shingles due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with shingles.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are used to create creams for shingles because of their analgesic and antiviral properties, which help reduce the severity of shingles symptoms.
Buds: Zingiber officinale buds are used in creams for shingles due to their ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, making them a potential natural remedy for shingles relief.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 50 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil in separate containers for melting.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler at 180-190 degrees fahrenheit for 10-15 minutes.
Combine the melted beeswax and coconut oil in a heat-proof mixing bowl and stir until uniform.
Add 10 grams of zingiber officinale root powder and 5 grams of vitamin e oil to the mixture.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30-40 minutes before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, reduces inflammation and swelling associated with shingles rash, providing relief from itching, burning, and discomfort.
Antiviral properties: Curcumin has been shown to inhibit the replication of the varicella-zoster virus (VZV), which causes shingles, helping to shorten the duration and severity of the outbreak.

Wound-healing properties: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties promote wound healing by reducing scarring, improving skin elasticity, and accelerating the repair process, which can help reduce post-shingles nerve pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The active compound curcumin in Curcuma longa has been shown to reduce the severity and duration of shingles symptoms, such as pain and itching. It also helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected areas. Additionally, curcumin has been found to have antiviral properties that can help to combat the varicella-zoster virus, the cause of shingles. This makes Curcuma longa creams a potential natural remedy for shingles relief.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin is a potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compound that helps to reduce pain, inflammation, and itching associated with shingles by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Tumerone: Tumerone is a sesquiterpene compound with anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that helps to reduce the severity and duration of shingles symptoms by inhibiting the replication of the varicella-zoster virus.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC is a curcuminoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that helps to reduce pain and inflammation associated with shingles by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, and also has antiviral activity against the varicella-zoster virus.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Curcuma longa are used because they contain high concentrations of curcumin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help alleviate shingles symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa are used because they contain a rich source of curcumin, which has been shown to reduce pain and inflammation associated with shingles.
Seeds: The seeds of Curcuma longa are not primarily used, however, the plant's seeds are not typically used in skincare products, but the seeds of other plants are often used in skincare products.
remedy_recipe: Grate 10-15g of dried curcuma longa root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar.
Combine the grated powder with 20g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture constantly for 5 minutes over low heat until the beeswax has melted completely.
Add 30g of shea butter and 10g of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir until smooth.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool and solidify for about 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Arnica's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as helenin and chamazulene, reduce swelling and pain associated with shingles by reducing inflammation and promoting blood flow to affected areas.
Antiviral: Mountain arnica contains antiviral properties that can help combat the varicella-zoster virus (VZV) that causes shingles, reducing the severity and duration of the outbreak.
Wound healing: Arnica's natural antibacterial and antifungal agents promote wound healing by cleansing the affected area, preventing infection, and stimulating collagen production to reduce scarring.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arnica montana, also known as mountain arnica, creams helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The herb contains compounds that reduce swelling, pain, and inflammation associated with shingles. Topical application of Arnica creams can help soothe the skin, accelerate healing, and reduce the duration of shingle outbreaks. Additionally, Arnica's antiviral properties help combat the herpes zoster virus, which causes shingles, promoting a faster recovery and relief from discomfort. This natural remedy can provide effective relief from shingle symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenes: These compounds, specifically helenalin and 11-oxohelenalin, have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help to reduce the severity and duration of shingles symptoms.

Flavonoids (specifically, isorhapontigenin and acacetin): These compounds exhibit antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which aid in reducing the viral load of the varicella-zoster virus, alleviating pain, and promoting wound healing in shingles patients.

Licochalcone A: This flavonoid compound has potent antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to inhibit the replication of the varicella-zoster virus, reduce inflammation, and alleviate pain associated with shingles.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Leaves: Leaves are sometimes used as a substitute for flowers, containing similar compounds and being used for their anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Roots: Roots of Arnica montana are occasionally used, but less frequently than flowers or leaves, and are valued for their slightly different composition of sesquiterpene lactones.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried arnica montana flowers from a reputable source or grow your own.
Combine the dried flowers with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2-3 hours or until the oil has turned a light greenish color.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Add 20 grams of beeswax to the oil mixture and heat it gently until the wax has melted.

remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow creams contain mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that soothe and calm irritated skin, providing relief from the burning and itching associated with shingles.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal marshmallow creams often contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain caused by shingles.

Antiviral properties: Some herbal marshmallow creams may exhibit antiviral activity, which can help combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles, reducing the severity of symptoms and duration of the outbreak.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, creams helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The roots of the plant contain mucilages, which form a protective barrier on the skin, reducing itching and discomfort associated with shingles. By providing a cooling and calming effect, Althaea officinalis creams can help alleviate the painful rash and promote the healing process. This natural remedy can also help to reduce stress and promote relaxation, making it an effective complementary treatment for shingles.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds may help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with shingles by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators.

Phenolic acids (e.g., caffeic acid, ferulic acid): These antioxidants may help mitigate oxidative stress and promote wound healing, which can aid in reducing the severity and duration of shingles symptoms.

Mucilages (e.g., galacturonic acid, rhamnose): The soothing and protective properties of mucilages may help calm irritated skin, reduce itching, and promote the healing of skin lesions associated with shingles.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high mucilage content, which helps to soothe and protect the skin.
Leaves: The leaves are also used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with shingles.
Flowers: The flowers are occasionally used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in promoting healing and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of althaea officinalis roots in late summer to early fall when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm oven at 50°c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Create a decoction by simmering 20g of dried roots in 500ml of distilled water for 10 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then allow it to cool to room temperature.
Mix 50g of coconut oil with the cooled decoction in a bowl and whip until well combined.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain creams helps with shingles because they possess anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and soothing properties that effectively alleviate the painful symptoms of this viral infection. The cream's active compounds, such as allantoin and apigenin, work together to reduce itching, burning, and blistering associated with shingles. Additionally, plantain creams can help accelerate the healing process by promoting collagen synthesis, improving skin elasticity, and reducing scarring. As a result, plantain creams provide natural relief from the discomfort and distress caused by shingles.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain creams contain compounds like aucubin and platonin, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce pain, swelling, and itching associated with shingles.
Antiviral properties: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in plantain creams may help combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles, reducing its ability to replicate and spread.

Sotheing and protective properties: Plantain's soothing and emollient effects can help calm the skin and provide a protective barrier against further irritation or infection, promoting faster healing and recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing skin irritations associated with shingles.
Barks: The bark has been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including itching and rashes caused by shingles, due to its astringent and antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean dried plantago major leaves measure 250 grams.
Grind the dried leaves into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered plantago major with 100 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally.
Strain and cool the mixture then pour into sterilized jars storing at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with shingles because they contain a natural compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The cream's active ingredients, such as valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, work together to soothe the affected area, reducing itching, burning, and discomfort associated with shingles. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to promote healing, shortening the duration of the outbreak and reducing the severity of symptoms. As a result, herbal valerian creams have become a popular natural remedy for relieving shingles pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Valerian root's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce swelling, pain, and discomfort associated with shingles rash, making it a potential natural remedy for soothing skin irritations.

Antiviral property: Valerian has been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against certain viruses, including herpes simplex virus (which causes shingles), which may help reduce the severity and duration of the outbreak.

Sedative and analgesic property: The sedative and analgesic compounds in valerian cream can help alleviate itching, numbness, and burning sensations often experienced during a shingles outbreak, promoting relaxation and comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of valerian are rich in valerenic acid, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, making them effective in relieving pain and itching associated with shingles.
Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes contain valerin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritation caused by shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots and dry them in a cool dark place for 2 weeks to preserve potency.
Grind 50 grams of dried valeriana officinalis roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground valeriana powder with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil and 20 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax has melted and the mixture is smooth.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to room temperature, then whip it with a whisk or electric mixer until it thickens into a creamy consistency suitable for topical application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow creams helps with shingles because they harness the natural anti-inflammatory properties of salicin, a compound found in white willow bark. This potent ingredient mimics the effects of aspirin, reducing pain and inflammation associated with shingles. The topical application of white willow cream directly targets the affected area, providing rapid relief from itching, burning, and numbness. Additionally, the soothing properties of white willow help to calm skin irritations, promoting a faster healing process and reducing the risk of scarring.
remedy_properties: Salicin: Salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, helps reduce pain, inflammation, and fever associated with shingles by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are hormones that cause pain and swelling.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in white willow creams help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate shingle symptoms like itching, burning, and blisters.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The anti-inflammatory compounds in white willow creams, such as salicin and polyphenols, help reduce inflammation and alleviate the discomfort associated with shingles, including burning, tingling, and numbness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps alleviate pain and inflammation associated with shingles.
Bark: Rich in flavonoids and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, useful for soothing and calming affected skin areas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest salix alba leaves at dawn when they are most potent and rich in salicin.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 8 hours.
Mix dried salix alba leaves with 250ml of cold-pressed coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the mixture in a double boiler at 70°c for 12 hours to extract salicin and other compounds.
Strain and filter the infused mixture through a cheesecloth and bottle it when cooled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with shingles because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as eucalyptol and cineole, work to soothe the skin and reduce itching, pain, and discomfort associated with shingle outbreaks. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties help to prevent infection and promote healing, reducing the duration and severity of symptoms. This natural remedy provides an effective alternative for managing shingles, offering relief from the uncomfortable and sometimes painful rash.
remedy_properties: Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol, a main component of Tasmanian Blue Gum (Eucalyptus globulus), has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling associated with shingles.
Cineole: Cineole, another key compound in the oil, exhibits antiviral properties that can help combat the varicella-zoster virus responsible for causing shingles, reducing its spread and severity.
Tannins: Tasmanian Blue Gum cream's tannin content has astringent properties that help reduce itching, discomfort, and inflammation caused by shingles, providing relief to affected areas.
remedy_parts: Barks: Containing terpenoids and flavonoids, the barks have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the skin affected by shingles.
Roots: Rich in saponins and alkaloids, the roots have antiviral properties that help combat the varicella-zoster virus responsible for shingles.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:2 for 6 hours under low heat.
Filter the infused oil using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard solids within 30 minutes.
Combine the filtered oil with beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler, melting at medium heat until mixture reaches 180°f within 20 minutes.
Add vitamin e oil and aloe vera gel to the melted mixture, stirring well to combine and allowing it to cool slightly for 10 minutes.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized containers, sealing tightly and refrigerating for a minimum of 24 hours before use within a month.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with shingles because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The menthol in peppermint cream can help reduce pain, itching, and burning sensations associated with shingles rash, while also relieving tension and stress that can exacerbate symptoms. Additionally, peppermint's antiviral compounds may help shorten the duration and severity of shingles outbreaks by inhibiting the replication of the herpes virus. Overall, herbal peppermint creams offer a natural and effective way to alleviate the discomfort and distress caused by shingles.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Peppermint's antiviral compounds, such as menthol and menthone, help reduce the replication of the varicella-zoster virus (VZV) that causes shingles, thereby alleviating symptoms.

Analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties: Menthol and other constituents in peppermint creams can numb the skin, reducing pain and discomfort associated with shingles, while also decreasing inflammation and swelling.

Cooling and soothing properties: The menthol and camphor present in peppermint creams have a cooling effect that can help reduce itching, burning, and stinging sensations commonly experienced during shingles outbreaks.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, peppermint roots help soothe and calm shingle-related nerve pain.
Barks: Peppermint barks contain salicylic acid, which reduces inflammation and eases itching, making it an effective ingredient in shingle creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh peppermint leaves and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Combine 250 grams of the cleaned peppermint leaves with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a stainless steel pot.
Steep the peppermint leaves for exactly 20 minutes, then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another container.
Mix the strained liquid with 150 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler set at medium heat.
Continue heating the mixture for precisely 15 minutes or until it reaches a temperature of 65 degrees celsius.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with shingles because it has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that soothe and calm the skin, reducing the discomfort and itching associated with shingle outbreaks. The cream's ability to reduce inflammation also helps to alleviate pain and numbness caused by nerve damage, allowing the skin to heal more quickly. Additionally, lemon balm has been shown to boost the immune system, helping the body fight off the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Lemon balm cream's antiviral properties help to reduce the replication of the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles, thereby alleviating symptoms and shortening the duration of the outbreak.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in lemon balm cream, such as rosmarinic acid, help to reduce inflammation and soothe the skin, which can alleviate the discomfort, pain, and itching associated with shingles rash.

Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Lemon balm's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and sedative properties can help to promote relaxation, reduce stress levels, and improve sleep quality, which are often disrupted by shingles symptoms such as pain, itching, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm contain high levels of rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can help soothe and reduce pain associated with shingles.
Barks: The bark of lemon balm contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in shingles wounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves from a trusted source, ensuring proper identification and cleanliness.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°f for 24 hours, or by air drying for 7-10 days.
Combine 20g of dried melissa officinalis leaves with 100ml of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a double boiler, heating gently for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, discarding solids and reserving the infused oil, which will take approximately 1 hour.
Add 10% beeswax by weight to the infused oil, melting at 180°f for 10-15 minutes, then pour into a sterilized cream base or container, allowing it to cool and solidify fully.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with shingles because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The cream's active compounds, such as terpenes and flavonoids, work to reduce pain, itching, and inflammation associated with shingles, while also combating the herpes zoster virus that causes the condition. By targeting both symptoms and the underlying infection, sage creams provide comprehensive relief for individuals suffering from shingles, helping to soothe and calm affected areas and promote healing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with shingles.

Antiviral properties: Sage contains antiviral compounds like camphor and borneol, which inhibit the replication of herpes viruses, including those responsible for shingles, reducing the severity and duration of outbreaks.

Pain-relieving and soothing properties: Sage's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties provide relief from the intense itching, burning, and shooting pain characteristic of shingles, while its antibacterial properties help prevent secondary infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sage plant are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective for soothing and calming the skin affected by shingles.
Bark: The bark of the sage plant contains bioflavonoids that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with shingles, making it an ideal ingredient for topical creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest salvia officinalis leaves from a trusted source ensuring freshness and potency.
Combine 20g of dried salvia officinalis leaves with 100ml of cold-pressed olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to ensure even infusion, at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar discarding solids and retaining the infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 10ml of coconut oil, heating gently until melted and blended for a smooth cream preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with shingles because it contains compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These properties help to reduce pain, itching, and inflammation associated with shingles rash, allowing for faster healing and reduced scarring. Additionally, thyme's antiviral properties may help to combat the virus responsible for shingles, reducing its replication and spread. This holistic approach can provide relief from the discomfort and distress caused by shingles, promoting overall well-being and a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Thyme's antiviral compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help combat the herpes virus that causes shingles, reducing its replication and spread.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory agents, like flavonoids and phenolic acids, soothe and calm the skin, reducing pain, itching, and discomfort associated with shingles rashes.
Antibacterial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds prevent bacterial overgrowth and secondary infections that can occur when the skin is broken or scratched during a shingles outbreak.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including shingles, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Barks: Thyme barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce inflammation, pain, and itching associated with shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and stems from the garden or store-bought dried thyme.
Dry the thyme in a warm dark place for two weeks or using a dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours.
Combine 20g of dried thymus vulgaris with 200ml of sweet almond oil and 50ml of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes at 100°c, stirring occasionally, to infuse the oil with thyme's active compounds.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before whipping the mixture into a smooth cream consistency.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for shingles are topical treatments infused with natural herbs and ingredients that help alleviate the symptoms of shingles, a viral infection caused by the herpes zoster virus. Examples of herbal creams that help include those containing aloe vera, tea tree oil, and capsaicin, which reduce itching, inflammation, and pain. By using these creams, people can experience relief from shingles and improve their quality of life, reducing discomfort and promoting faster healing.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to shingles that are treated with herbal creams are herpes simplex, cold sores, and genital warts. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as tea tree oil, aloe vera, and chamomile have anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties that help soothe and heal these skin conditions. These natural remedies can reduce pain, itching, and inflammation associated with these ailments, providing an effective alternative to conventional treatments.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with shingles is Aloe vera and tea tree oil-based creams. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and itching, while its soothing effects help to calm the affected skin. Tea tree oil, rich in antiviral compounds, aids in combating the shingles virus and accelerating the healing process. Additionally, incorporating creams containing witch hazel and calendula can enhance the soothing and anti-inflammatory effects, promoting faster recovery and reducing discomfort associated with shingles.
